title: Software Engineer (Java, React & Python)
description:

Our dynamic and diversified firm spans asset management, wealth management, and fintech, offering many ways to help investors make progress toward their goals. Our talented teams working around the globe bring expertise that’s both broad and unique. **Come join us in delivering better outcomes for our clients around the world!**
 The Digital Assets Technology team at Franklin Templeton is looking for a full stack developer to support innovative projects of small to large scale with a focus on our security protocols. Our team is focused on delivering technology to represent shares in mutual funds with tokens issued on blockchain platform(s) in a manner which permits live trading/exchange on a potentially worldwide and 24/7 basis. We support a mobile app focused on retail investors, a website focused on institutional investors and an internal admin tool for our customer support team. We have a variety of projects to enhance the current platform as well as projects to expand our reach outside the US.

* You will create application designs, data models, and interface specifications.

* You will support integration testing and technology roadmap planning.


***What will help you be successful in this role?***
**Experience \\& Capabilities**
* Strong communication and collaboration skills
* Ability to work independently and think critically
* Structured problem‑solving using available resources
* Experience supporting financial reconciliation systems
* Interest or exposure to blockchain technology


**Technical Skills**
* Java, React, and Python development experience
* Experience with Kubernetes orchestration
* Experience with external file transmissions and integration standards
* Familiarity with Atlassian tools


**Job Level \\- Individual Contributor**
**Work Shift Timings \\- 5:30 PM \\- 2:30 AM IST**
******Experience our welcoming culture and reach your professional and personal potential!******
 Our culture is shaped by our diverse global workforce and strongly held core values. Regardless of your interests, lifestyle, or background, there’s a place for you at Franklin Templeton. We provide employees with the tools, resources, and learning opportunities to help them excel in their career and personal life.

 By joining us, you will become part of a culture that focuses on employee well\\-being and provides multidimensional support for a positive and healthy lifestyle. We understand that benefits are at the core of employee well\\-being and may vary depending on individual needs. Whether you need support for maintaining your physical and mental health, saving for life’s adventures, taking care of your family members, or making a positive impact in your community, we aim to have them covered.
   

  

**Highlights Of Our Benefits Include**

title: Software Engineer
description:

 Essential Functions

* Remain self\\-driven and eager to learn the IAM technology stack, adapting quickly to new tools and approaches.

 This is a hybrid position. Expectation of days in the office will be confirmed by your Hiring Manager.
   

  

  

  




**Qualifications**

 Basic Qualifications:
   

  





 Bachelor's degree, OR 3\\+ years of relevant work experience
   

  





 Preferred Qualifications:
   

  





 Bachelor’s degree OR 3\\+ years of relevant work experience.
   

  





 2 or more years of work experience.
   

  





 1\\+ years of hands\\-on programming experience in Python, Java, Spring boot, solid computer\\-science fundamentals, including data structures, algorithms, and operating system concepts.
   

  





 Knowledge of Linux ecosystem and experience with bash scripting.
   

  





 Hands\\-on development experience with the following IAM tool – SailPoint, is highly desirable.
   

  





 Exposure to AI/ML techniques, AI Agents, Model Context Protocol (MCP) and GenAI\\-assisted development platforms (e.g., GitHub Copilot, Cline, Claude code), with an interest in applying AI to code generation, log analysis, anomaly detection, and security automation.
   

  





 Understanding of security principles, including zero trust architecture and privileged access management.
   

  





 Familiarity with web\\-service technologies (REST), Application programming interfaces (APIs), JavaScript frameworks, and SQL/relational databases.
   

  





 Experience troubleshooting applications and services in complex, distributed network environments, strong analytical skills for diagnosing and resolving multifaceted technical issues.

 Demonstrated aptitude for quickly learning new programming languages, security tools, and infrastructure technologies, with a keen interest in emerging fields such as AI, ML, and cybersecurity automation.
   

  

  

  




**Additional Information**

title: System Application Engineer
description:
System Application Engineer (Staff/Senior Staff )
   

  

 Location: Bengaluru , India
   

  

**Role Overview**
 As a System Application Engineer (staff/senior staff) you will be the technical face of our next\\-generation Gas Sensing Solution employing our state of the art MEMS Sensor and Analog Front\\-End (AFE) chip family. You will take ownership in translating high\\-level customer problems into complete, production\\-ready reference designs. Drawing on deep mixed\\-signal expertise, you will create evaluation boards, application notes, design tools and in\\-field demos that accelerate customer design\\-in and showcase our Gas Sensor \\+ AFE SoC’s competitive edge. Your insights will directly shape product and silicon road\\-maps and go\\-to\\-market collateral
   

  

**Key Responsibilities**
* Own the reference design strategy where you will define, architect and deliver PCB \\+ firmware reference designs that solve real customer use\\-cases for Gas Sensing Applications.
* Engage with end\\-customers to distil application requirements, performance gaps and emerging trends.
* Collateral creation – author application notes, user guides, BOMs, schematics, simulation models and embedded drivers
* Design, bring\\-up and validate evaluation boards; implement automated test scripts for performance verification
* Capture field learnings, benchmark competition, and feed quantitative data back to marketing \\& design
* Present at webinars, industry forums and author technical blogs to position our Gas Sensing Solution as the reference platform


**What We’re Looking For**
* B.Tech/M.Tech/Ph.D. in EE/ECE with 8\\+ years experience in system applications engineering for mixed\\-signal ICs and sensing solutions. with at least one end\\-to\\-end AFE, data\\-acquisition or sensor\\-interface product in volume production
* Proven track record creating reference designs/eval boards and associated collateral for tier\\-1 customers
* Expert knowledge of analog signal\\-chains (ADC/DAC, PGAs, low\\-noise amps, sensor excitation), embedded MCU/RISC\\-V or ARM subsystems, and precision power supplies
* Hands\\-on proficiency with PCB design, lab instrumentation, scripting (Python/LabVIEW), and C/C\\+\\+ firmware
* Strong customer\\-facing communication skills and ability to convey complex trade\\-offs to both executives and design engineers
* Experience in industrial or automotive sensor systems with strict EMC/EMI and safety compliance


**Nice To Have**
* Familiarity with IEC 61010, IEC 60079, ISO 13485 or IEC 61508, ISO 26262 functional\\-safety flows
* Knowledge of sensor technologies (e.g., MEMS, piezoelectric, or capacitive sensors).
* Digital signal processing (DSP) , AI/ML techniques for sensor data processing.


 Why Join Us?
   

  

* Lead the system\\-level vision for a Gas Sensing Solutions that will anchor multiple vertical platforms.
* Influence product and silicon road\\-maps while remaining deeply hands\\-on with hardware, firmware and real customer problems.
* Be a key player in an innovative Indian startup shaping the future of sensor technology.
* Competitive compensation, ESOP , and a vibrant work culture.

title: Data Engineer
description:
**About The Company**
 Turing is a pioneering technology company headquartered in San Francisco, California, renowned for its leadership in frontier artificial intelligence research and development. As a research accelerator, Turing collaborates with some of the world's most innovative AI labs, providing cutting\\-edge solutions and insights that drive the future of AI technology. The company is committed to supporting global enterprises in deploying advanced AI systems by offering high\\-quality data, sophisticated training pipelines, and expert AI researchers specializing in coding, reasoning, multilinguality, multimodality, and autonomous agents. Turing's mission is to transform AI from experimental proof\\-of\\-concept to reliable, proprietary intelligence that delivers measurable business impact and sustained growth for its clients.
   

  

**About The Role**
 We are seeking experienced Software Engineers (SWE Bench – Data Engineer / Data Science) to join our dynamic team. In this role, you will work on benchmark\\-driven evaluation projects that focus on real\\-world data engineering and data science workflows. Your primary responsibility will be to handle production\\-like datasets, develop and optimize data pipelines, and perform data analysis to evaluate and enhance the performance of advanced AI systems. You will collaborate closely with researchers and engineers to design challenging data tasks, ensuring that benchmarking processes are robust, reproducible, and aligned with real\\-world applications.
   

  

 This position offers an excellent opportunity for professionals with a strong foundation in data engineering and data science to contribute to high\\-impact projects in a fast\\-paced, innovative environment. Your work will directly influence the development and deployment of AI systems used across various industries, helping to push the boundaries of what AI can achieve.
   

  

**Qualifications**
 The ideal candidate will possess a minimum of three years of relevant experience as a Data Engineer, Data Scientist, or Software Engineer with a focus on data workflows. Proficiency in Python is essential, alongside demonstrable experience in data processing, analysis, and model\\-related workflows. Candidates should have a solid understanding of machine learning fundamentals and experience working with both structured and unstructured data. Strong problem\\-solving skills, the ability to navigate complex codebases, and a commitment to writing clean, maintainable, and well\\-documented code are critical. Excellent communication skills in English, both spoken and written, are also required to facilitate effective collaboration within multidisciplinary teams.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
* Work with structured and unstructured datasets to support evaluation tasks aligned with SWE Bench standards.
* Design, develop, and validate data pipelines used in benchmarking and evaluation workflows.
* Perform comprehensive data processing, feature engineering, analysis, and validation to support data science initiatives.
* Write, test, and modify Python scripts for data processing and experiment support in local environments.
* Assess data quality, transformations, and outputs to ensure accuracy, reproducibility, and reliability.
* Create clean, well\\-documented, and reusable data workflows suitable for benchmarking purposes.
* Participate in code reviews to uphold high standards of code quality, readability, and maintainability.
* Collaborate with multidisciplinary teams to design complex, real\\-world data engineering and data science tasks for AI evaluation.


**Benefits**
 Joining Turing as a freelancer offers the flexibility of working remotely from anywhere, providing a balanced work\\-life environment. You will have the opportunity to engage with cutting\\-edge AI projects, collaborating with leading companies in the language model and AI space. This role allows you to expand your expertise, contribute to innovative solutions, and be part of a global network of talented professionals. Additionally, Turing provides support and resources to help you succeed in your freelance journey, including access to a vibrant community and ongoing learning opportunities.
   

  

 Equal Opportunity
   

  

 Turing is committed to fostering an inclusive and diverse work environment. We provide equal employment opportunities to all applicants regardless of race, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, national origin, disability, or veteran status. We believe that diverse perspectives and backgrounds drive innovation and excellence, and we are dedicated to creating a workplace where everyone can thrive and contribute to our mission of advancing AI technology.

title: Controls & Automation Engineer
description:
**About Anzo Controls Pvt. Ltd.**

  





 With a strong vision to position India as a global hub for robotics and automation, Anzo Controls Pvt. Ltd. develops cutting\\-edge indigenous solutions across industrial automation. From SCARA robots to AGVs/AMRs, Delta robots, gantry systems, ASRS, and complete material handling solutions, we are committed to innovation under the philosophy of
 **“Make in India, Build for India.”** 




 Backed by a multidisciplinary team of 100\\+ professionals across engineering, manufacturing, and software, Anzo Controls is rapidly expanding its capabilities in robotics, motion control, and smart automation systems.
 



  




**Role Overview**

  





 We are seeking a
 **Controls \\& Automation Engineer** 
 with strong expertise in
 **robotics, servo drives, and motion control systems** 
 . The candidate will be responsible for developing, implementing, and optimizing control systems for advanced automation solutions including robotic systems, material handling equipment, and special\\-purpose machines.
 



 This role requires hands\\-on experience in PLC programming, motion control tuning, and integration of robotics with industrial automation systems.
 



  




**Key Roles \\& Responsibilities**

  




* Design, develop, and implement
 **control systems** 
 for automation projects including robotics, conveyors, AGVs/AMRs, and material handling systems.
* Program and troubleshoot
 **PLC, HMI, and SCADA systems** 
 for industrial automation applications.
* Configure, tune, and optimize
 **servo drives and motion control systems** 
 for precision applications.
* Integrate and commission
 **robotic systems** 
 such as SCARA, Delta, Gantry, and articulated robots.
* Develop and manage
 **multi\\-axis motion control applications** 
 including synchronization, interpolation, and path control.
* Work closely with
 **mechanical, electrical, and software teams** 
 for system integration and project execution.
* Perform
 **machine commissioning, testing, and troubleshooting** 
 at shop floor and customer sites.
* Ensure smooth communication between hardware components using
 **industrial communication protocols** 
 (Ethernet/IP, Profinet, Modbus, etc.).
* Create and maintain
 **technical documentation** 
 , including control schematics, IO lists, and user manuals.
* Support
 **new product development** 
 and customized automation solutions based on client requirements.
* Participate in
 **continuous improvement and optimization** 
 of existing automation systems.



  




**Key Skills \\& Expertise**

  




* Strong experience in
 **PLC programming and troubleshooting** 
 (Siemens / Allen Bradley / Mitsubishi preferred).
* Hands\\-on expertise in
 **servo drives, VFDs, and motion controllers** 
 .
* Solid understanding of
 **robotics systems and integration** 
 .
* Experience in
 **multi\\-axis motion control and synchronization** 
 .
* Proficiency in
 **HMI and SCADA development** 
 .
* Knowledge of
 **industrial automation hardware, control panels, and electrical systems** 
 .
* Familiarity with
 **industrial communication protocols** 
 (Ethernet/IP, Profinet, Modbus, CAN, etc.).
* Strong analytical and
 **problem\\-solving skills** 
 .
* Ability to work in a
 **cross\\-functional, fast\\-paced environment** 
 .



  




**Experience \\& Qualifications**

  




* **1–4 years of experience** 
 in controls and automation, preferably in robotics or material handling industry.
* Hands\\-on experience in
 **robotics and motion control applications is mandatory** 
 .
* Bachelor’s degree in
 **Electrical / Electronics / Instrumentation / Automation Engineering** 
 or related field.
* Experience in
 **machine commissioning and field support** 
 is an added advantage.
* Willingness to travel for
 **site installation and commissioning** 
 when required.

title: Adobe Campaign Developer
description:

 Choosing Capgemini means choosing a company where you will be empowered to shape your career in the way you’d like, where you’ll be supported and inspired by a collaborative community of colleagues around the world, and where you’ll be able to reimagine what’s possible. Join us and help the world’s leading organizations unlock the value of technology and build a more sustainable, more inclusive world.
 



  





**Your Role** 




 As an Adobe Campaign Classic Support Specialist, you will be responsible for ensuring the smooth functioning, maintenance, and support of Adobe Campaign Classic platforms for our customers. You will act as the primary point of contact for production issues, system upkeep, and integration\\-related activities.
 



  





**In this role you will play a key role in:** 



* Supporting and maintaining customers’ Adobe Campaign Classic platform.
* Handling tickets in the production environment and ensuring timely resolution.
* Performing Root Cause Analysis (RCA) on recurring issues and implementing permanent fixes.
* Monitoring workflows and taking corrective actions proactively.
* Coordinating system\\-related activities between users and multiple technical teams.
* Maintaining Adobe Campaign application servers and coordinating with DBAs.
* Understanding and managing integration of Adobe Campaign with other applications.



  





**Your Profile** 




 You are a detail\\-oriented professional with strong technical expertise in Adobe Campaign Classic and excellent problem\\-solving skills. You can work independently, communicate effectively, and collaborate with cross\\-functional teams.
 



  





**Top 5 Technical Skills** 



1. Adobe Campaign Classic (Neolane) – Support and development experience (4–5 years).
2. Adobe Campaign Modules – Message Centre, Content Manager, FDA.
3. Programming Languages – HTML, XML, JavaScript, SQL.
4. Integration Expertise – Adobe Campaign with other systems.
5. Basic Unix \\& SQL Knowledge – For troubleshooting and maintenance.



  





**Job Description** 



* Provide support and maintenance for Adobe Campaign Classic platforms.
* Handle production tickets and ensure timely resolution.
* Perform RCA on recurring issues and implement permanent fixes.
* Monitor workflows and take corrective actions.
* Coordinate system\\-related activities between users and technical teams.
* Maintain Adobe Campaign application servers and work with DBAs.

* Work directly with end customers; willingness to travel if required.



  





**Additional Notes:** 



* 4–5 years of support and development experience with Adobe Campaign Classic (Neolane).

* Knowledge of HTML, XML, JavaScript, SQL.

* Good communication and interpersonal skills.



  





**What you'll love about working here** 



* You can shape your career with us. We offer a range of career paths and internal opportunities within Capgemini group. You will also get personalized career guidance from our leaders.
* You will get comprehensive wellness benefits including health checks, telemedicine, insurance with top\\-ups, elder care, partner coverage or new parent support via flexible work.
* At Capgemini, you can work on cutting\\-edge projects in tech and engineering with industry leaders or create solutions to overcome societal and environmental challenges.



  





 Capgemini is a global business and technology transformation partner, helping organizations to accelerate their dual transition to a digital and sustainable world, while creating tangible impact for enterprises and society. It is a responsible and diverse group of 340,000 team members in more than 50 countries. With its strong over 55\\-year heritage, Capgemini is trusted by its clients to unlock the value of technology to address the entire breadth of their business needs. It delivers end\\-to\\-end services and solutions leveraging strengths from strategy and design to engineering, all fueled by its market leading capabilities in AI, generative AI, cloud and data, combined with its deep industry expertise and partner ecosystem.

title: Software Engineer - L3 Support
description:
Canonical is a leading provider of open source software and operating systems to the global enterprise and technology markets. Our platform, Ubuntu, is very widely used in breakthrough enterprise initiatives such as public cloud, data science, AI, engineering innovation, and IoT. Our customers include the world's leading public cloud and silicon providers, and industry leaders in many sectors. The company is a pioneer of global distributed collaboration, with 1200\\+ colleagues in 75\\+ countries and very few office\\-based roles. Teams meet two to four times yearly in person, in interesting locations around the world, to align on strategy and execution.
   

  

 The company is founder\\-led, profitable, and growing.
   

  

 We are hiring a Software Engineer \\- L3 Support to…
   

  

 …work in a dynamic and exciting engineering role in Linux\\-based infrastructure and applications, covering all layers of the stack, including bare metal, virtualization (KVM), containerization (Docker/LXC/LXD), storage (Ceph and Linux filesystems), networking (OVS, OVN and Core networking), OpenStack, Kubernetes and the open source applications running on top of them. It will challenge you to show the breadth of your engineering skills, which will be needed to work on almost any aspect of Ubuntu and the open source applications large enterprise customers run.
   

  

 This role is an opportunity for a technologist with a passion for Linux and open source to build a career with Canonical and drive success for our customers, community and the company. If you have an affinity for open source software, great communication skills, and a passion for troubleshooting and fixing issues in technology used by millions across the world, then you will enjoy working with some of the best people in the industry at Canonical.
   

  

 This role deals with critical issues in the open source stack that require upstream bug fixes. Our engineers are able to work productively at any level of the stack including the kernel and in a wide range of languages, to understand and address the software issues at hand. Our group is critical to the success of our customers, partners and Ubuntu itself.
   

  

 You will help with troubleshooting and driving issues to resolution with workarounds, guidance, and bug fixes to be released upstream and in Ubuntu.
   

  

**Location:** 
 This is a remote role, we have teams in all time zones.
   

  

 The role entails
   

  

* Resolve complex customer problems related to Ubuntu, Kernel, Ceph, OpenStack, or Kubernetes and other open source software
* Maintain a close working relationship with Canonical's Field, Support and product engineering teams
* Participate in upstream communities
* Develop bug fixes, backport patches, and work with upstream for inclusion
* Review code produced by other engineers
* Demonstrate good judgment in technical methods and techniques
* Prioritize work and manage your time effectively against those priorities
* Participate in team discussions to improve processes, tools, and documentation
* Maintain clear, technical and concise communications
* Work from home and travel internationally up to 10% of work time for team meetings, events and conferences


 What we are looking for in you
   

  

* An exceptional academic track record
* Background in Computer Science, STEM or similar
* Experience with Linux and open source software
* Experience with at least one of Python, Go, C or C\\+\\+ on Linux
* A drive to learn unfamiliar technology and deep\\-dive difficult issues
* Willingness to travel up to 4 times a year for internal events


 Nice\\-to\\-have skills
   

  

* You love technology and working with brilliant people
* You are curious, flexible, articulate, and accountable
* You value soft skills and are passionate, enterprising, thoughtful, and self\\-motivated
* You have interest in, or willingness to learn about any of the following: Ubuntu Linux \\- kernel or userspace, Kubernetes, OpenStack, Ceph, QEMU/KVM, LXC/LXD,Postgresql, Mongo, Debian packaging, distributed systems


 What we offer colleagues
   

  

 We consider geographical location, experience, and performance in shaping compensation worldwide. We revisit compensation annually (and more often for graduates and associates) to ensure we recognize outstanding performance. In addition to base pay, we offer a performance\\-driven annual bonus. We provide all team members with additional benefits, which reflect our values and ideals. We balance our programs to meet local needs and ensure fairness globally.
   

  

* Distributed work environment with twice\\-yearly team sprints in person
* Personal learning and development budget of USD 2,000 per year
* Annual compensation review
* Recognition rewards
* Annual holiday leave
* Maternity and paternity leave
* Employee Assistance Programme \\& Wellness Platform
* Opportunity to travel to new locations to meet colleagues
* Priority Pass and travel upgrades for long\\-haul company events


**About Canonical**
 Canonical is a pioneering tech firm at the forefront of the global move to open source. As the company that publishes Ubuntu, one of the most important open\\-source projects and the platform for AI, IoT, and the cloud, we are changing the world of software. We recruit on a global basis and set a very high standard for people joining the company. We expect excellence; in order to succeed, we need to be the best at what we do. Most colleagues at Canonical have worked from home since our inception in 2004\\. Working here is a step into the future and will challenge you to think differently, work smarter, learn new skills, and raise your game.
   

  

 Canonical is an equal opportunity employer
   

  

 We are proud to foster a workplace free from discrimination. Diversity of experience, perspectives, and background create a better work environment and better products. Whatever your identity, we will give your application fair consideration.

title: Sr Quality Assurance Engineer
description:
Powering the world’s payments ecosystem
   

 ACI powers the payments ecosystem – globally, and you power ACI. You’ll innovate, collaborate, and grow – in an energetic technology culture with decades of proven success. ACIers – in all roles and levels – are truly your colleagues and many are your friends. Our size and reach allow you to see the global impact of your work. You are visible, your talents are valued, and you are empowered to shape the future of payments.
   

 As a Sr Quality Assurance Engineer in Pune, you will join a diverse, passionate team, dedicated to powering the world’s payments ecosystem!
   

  

 The Sr Quality Assurance Engineer is responsible for ensuring the quality, reliability, and performance of software products through systematic testing and strong quality practices. This role partners closely with Engineering, Architecture, and Product teams to validate requirements, design test strategies, lead automation initiatives, and ensure end‑to‑end system quality across functional, integration, API, and performance testing.
   

  

  

  

  

 Key Responsibilities
   

  

 \\- Participate in requirements design and validation; identify test coverage, gaps, risks, and quality measures early in the project lifecycle.
   

  

 \\- Define and execute test strategies, test plans, and detailed test cases using appropriate estimation techniques.
   

  

 \\- Perform end‑to‑end functional, integration, and regression testing across complex systems and environments.
   

  

 \\- Design, develop, and maintain automation frameworks and scripts using Playwright and JavaScript, following Page Object Model (POM) and data‑driven framework principles.
   

  

 \\- Refactor and enhance automation frameworks to ensure reusability, scalability, and maintainability.
   

  

 \\- Validate REST APIs using Postman and Swagger UI.
   

  

 \\- Write and execute SQL queries to validate backend data and support defect analysis and troubleshooting.
   

  

 \\- Manage test execution, defect logging, analysis, and coordination with Development teams through resolution.
   

  

 \\- Support performance, load, and stress testing; analyze results and provide feedback on platform and application capabilities.
   

  

 \\- Prepare and manage complex test environments and test data.
   

  

 \\- Provide guidance, metrics, and quality insights to engineering, product, and project stakeholders.
   

  

 \\- Raise quality risks and issues through appropriate escalation channels and support on‑time delivery without compromising quality.
   

  

 \\- Adhere to all corporate policies, including the Code of Business Conduct and Ethics.
   

  

  

  

  

 Required Qualifications
   

  

 \\- Bachelors degree in Computer Science or related field, or equivalent experience.
   

  

 \\- 7\\+ years of experience in Quality Assurance and/or systems testing.
   

  

 \\- 3\\+ years of hands‑on test automation experience.
   

  

 \\- Strong experience with Playwright, JavaScript, POM, and data‑driven automation frameworks.
   

  

 \\- Experience with API testing (Postman, Swagger) and SQL for backend validation.
   

  

 \\- Experience with Test Management and Defect Management tools.
   

  

  

  

  

 Preferred Qualifications
   

  

 \\- Understanding of platform and operating system architecture.
   

  

 \\- Relevant software quality or testing certification.
   

  

  

  

  

 Work Environment
   

  

 \\- Standard work environment; majority of time spent on PC.
   

  

 \\- Travel may be required (domestic or international)
   

  

  

 Core Capabilities:
   

 We seek colleagues who embody our core capabilities — these shape our culture and enable us to make a meaningful impact together:
   

 \\- Ensure Accountability: holding self and others accountable to meet commitments.
   

  

 \\- Drives Results: consistently achieving results, even under tough circumstances.
   

  

 \\- Customer Focus: building strong customer relationships and delivering customer\\-centric solutions.
   

  

 \\- Cultivate Innovation: creating new and better ways for the organization to be successful.
   

  

 \\- Collaborates: building partnerships and working collaboratively with others.
   

  

 \\- Courage: stepping up to address difficult issues, saying what needs to be said.
   

  

  

 I n return for your expertise, we offer opportunities for growth, career development, and a competitive compensation and benefits package—all within an innovative and collaborative work environment.
   

 Are you ready to help us transform the payments ecosystem? To learn more about ACI Worldwide, visit our web site at www.aciworldwide.com

title: Android System Engineer
description:
**About The Company**
 Qualcomm Incorporated is a global leader in the semiconductor and telecommunications equipment industry, renowned for its innovative contributions to mobile technology and wireless communications. With a strong focus on research and development, Qualcomm develops and supplies integrated circuits, software, and services that enable the connected world. The company’s commitment to innovation, quality, and sustainability has established it as a trusted partner for device manufacturers, network operators, and consumers worldwide. Qualcomm’s extensive portfolio includes cutting\\-edge technologies such as 5G, IoT, automotive, and AI, positioning it at the forefront of technological evolution.
   

  

**About The Role**
 We are seeking a highly skilled Security Engineer to join our dynamic team at Qualcomm India Private Limited. In this role, you will be responsible for enabling security mitigation features and developing advanced tools to detect vulnerabilities within our products. Your expertise will contribute to strengthening the security posture of our offerings by working closely with cross\\-functional teams to understand security design reviews and provide innovative solutions. The ideal candidate will have a deep understanding of Android OS, Linux kernel internals, and embedded programming, with a passion for identifying and mitigating security risks in complex systems.
   

  

**Qualifications**
 The ideal candidate will possess a Bachelor’s, Master’s, or PhD degree in Engineering, Computer Science, Information Systems, or a related field from a reputed institution, with a strong academic record. Candidates should have at least 2\\-3 years of relevant work experience in software engineering, with proficiency in programming languages such as C, C\\+\\+, Java, and Python. Experience with security features, kernel development, and embedded systems is essential. Knowledge of ARMv8/ARMv9 security extensions, fuzzing tools, and Linux security frameworks will be considered advantageous. The candidate should demonstrate the ability to independently drive feature development and resolve complex issues effectively.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
 Your primary responsibilities will include developing and implementing security mitigation features at both kernel and user space levels. You will collaborate with technical teams to review security designs and provide alternative, robust solutions to enhance system security. The role involves working on Android OS, Linux kernel internals, and embedded platforms, ensuring the integration of security features without compromising system performance. You will also develop automation tools using scripting languages, conduct vulnerability assessments, and utilize fuzzing tools like Syzkaller, Trinity, or AFL to identify potential security flaws. Additionally, you will stay updated on the latest security threats and contribute to the continuous improvement of security protocols across products.
   

  

**Benefits**
 Qualcomm offers a comprehensive benefits package that includes competitive salary, health insurance, retirement plans, and paid time off. Employees have access to continuous learning opportunities, technical training, and career development programs. The company fosters a collaborative work environment that encourages innovation and professional growth. Additionally, Qualcomm promotes work\\-life balance through flexible work arrangements and wellness initiatives. Employees also benefit from a diverse and inclusive workplace culture that values different perspectives and experiences.
   

  

 Equal Opportunity
   

  

 Qualcomm is an equal opportunity employer committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. We do not discriminate based on race, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, national origin, disability, or veteran status. We are dedicated to providing reasonable accommodations to candidates with disabilities throughout the hiring process and in the workplace. Qualcomm values diversity and believes that a varied workforce enhances innovation and business success. We encourage all qualified individuals to apply and join our team in shaping the future of wireless technology.

title: Power Platform Developer (Senior)
description:
**WPP is the trusted growth partner for the world’s leading brands.**
**We unite cutting\\-edge media intelligence and data solutions, world\\-class creativity, next\\-generation production, transformative enterprise solutions and expert strategic counsel in a single company – powered by exceptional talent and our agentic marketing platform, WPP Open, to help our clients navigate change, capture opportunity and deliver transformational growth.**
**We have been building the world's most valuable brands for 50 years and have global reach across 100\\+ markets, with deep local expertise.**
**Our people are the key to our success. We're committed to fostering a culture of creativity, belonging and continuous learning, attracting and developing the brightest talent, and providing exciting career opportunities that help our people grow.**
**For more information, visit WPP.com.**
**Why we're hiring:**
 WPP is a creative transformation company, helping clients create growth by combining technology and creativity. As one of the world's largest marketing and advertising services organizations, we are committed to driving innovation and operational excellence across our vast global network. You will be joining the Automation Operations Centre within WPP's Enterprise Technology, Automation \\& AI department. This is a pivotal time for us as we're building a dedicated, in\\-house team to deliver, manage, monitor, and optimize our growing portfolio of automation solutions. This team is at the forefront of our digital transformation, ensuring the seamless operation and continuous improvement of critical automation capabilities across WPP globally
   

  

 You will play a crucial role in solution delivery and day\\-to\\-day technology management of the Global Automation Operations Centre (AOC) program. Reporting to the Automation Operations Manager and supporting. Automation product, platform and delivery teams worldwide, you will ensure our automation projects are well managed and supported to deliver quantifiable returns.
   

  

 The AOC is chartered to support automation products delivered by the Automation \\& AI product team, or projects delivered by Automation delivery teams from around WPP. Our support apparatus gives those teams confidence and freedom to hand over their existing successes and continue innovating.
   

  

 Are you a proactive and detail\\-oriented individual with a passion for technology and a strong desire to learn and grow in the field of automation \\& AI? We are looking for an L1 Automation Support Engineer to join our dynamic team. This entry\\-level role is perfect for someone eager to start their career in automation \\& AI support and operations, where you'll be responsible for the initial monitoring, triage, and resolution of incidents for our RPA bots and Power Platform solutions. You'll gain invaluable hands\\-on experience, working with cutting\\-edge technologies and collaborating with experienced engineers to ensure our global automation processes run smoothly.
   

  

**What you'll be doing:**
* Solution Development: Develop new automation solutions and extend existing products using a hybrid stack of RPA (UiPath/Microsoft) and Power Platform components.
* Technical Refinement: Lead the effort to adapt and refine existing automatic projects to ensure they meet evolving global business requirements and architectural standards.
* Platform Support: Deliver expert design, build and optimization for Microsoft Power Automate flows, Power Apps, and Dataverse\\-backed solution components.
* Data \\& Integration: Write and optimize SQL, FetchXML, and Dataverse API queries for data validation, to support intricate automation logic, and to efficiently troubleshoot workflow failures.
* API Management: Design efficient, high\\-performance Cloud Flows utilizing deep expertise in REST API integration, JSON manipulation, and XPath.
* Dependency Management: Proactively manage data sources, connections, and backend dependencies crucial for automation functionality.
* Updates \\& Upgrades: Support the deployment of updates, patches, and version upgrades for RPA tools and Power Platform components, ensuring minimal disruption.
* Collaboration \\& Documentation: Maintain comprehensive and up\\-to\\-date documentation for workflows, logic, and runbooks, while collaborating with Agile PMs and Engineering Leads to resolve technical blockers.


**What you'll need:**
* Overall experience: 7\\+ years of experience in automation operations, technology development.
* RPA \\& Power Platform: At least 4 years of dedicated experience specifically with RPA technologies (UiPath or Microsoft) and/or Power Platform.
* Technical Architecture: Expert proficiency in Typescript/Javascript, Dataverse architecture, Model\\-driven Apps, and Power Automate Online.
* Integration Expertise: Deep understanding of RESTful services, JSON schema, and complex data manipulation (XPath/Expressions) for efficient flow design.
* Database Proficiency: Advanced capability in using SQL, FetchXML, and Dataverse API for data validation and troubleshooting.
* Methodology: Proven experience as an Agile practitioner, specifically within Kanban or Scrum environments.
* Problem\\-solving: Proven experience dealing with technical blockers, run\\-time errors, and environment configuration challenges.
* Effective Communicator: Excellent writing and verbal communication skills for documenting technical logic and communicating with stakeholders.
* Attention to Detail: High level of accuracy in maintaining configuration items and troubleshooting guides.


**Mandatory Certifications**
* PL\\-400: Microsoft Power Platform Developer Associate OR
* PL\\-500: Microsoft Certified Power Automate RPA Developer Associate


**Preferred Certifications:**
* PL\\-400 or PL\\-500: (Whichever certification is not held as a mandatory requirement)
* GH\\-300: GitHub Copilot (Required within 3 months of joining)
* Automation Developer Professional: Relevant certification from a major RPA vendor


 like UiPath, Automation Anywhere, or Blue Prism
   

  

**Who you are:**
**You're open
 *:***
 We are inclusive and collaborative; we encourage the free exchange of ideas; we respect and celebrate diverse views. We are open\\-minded: to new ideas, new partnerships, new ways of working.
   

  

**You're optimistic
 *:***
 We believe in the power of creativity, technology and talent to create brighter futures or our people, our clients and our communities. We approach all that we do with conviction: to try the new and to seek the unexpected.
   

  

**You're extraordinary:** 
 we are stronger together: through collaboration we achieve the amazing. We are creative leaders and pioneers of our industry; we provide extraordinary every day.
   

  

**What we'll give you:**
**Passionate, inspired people** 
 – We aim to create a culture in which people can do extraordinary work.
   

  

**Scale and opportunity** 
 – We offer the opportunity to create, influence and complete projects at a scale that is unparalleled in the industry.
   

  

**Challenging and stimulating work** 
 – Unique work and the opportunity to join a group of creative problem solvers. Are you up for the challenge?
   

  

**We believe the best work happens when we're together, fostering creativity, collaboration, and connection. That's why we’ve adopted a hybrid approach, with teams in the office around four days a week. If you require accommodations or flexibility, please discuss this with the hiring team during the interview process.**

title: Embedded Linux Field Engineer for Devices/IoT
description:
**Job Description**
 Canonical is a leading provider of open source software and operating systems to the global enterprise and technology markets. Our platform, Ubuntu, is very widely used in breakthrough enterprise initiatives such as public cloud, data science, AI, engineering innovation and IoT. Our customers include the world's leading public cloud and silicon providers, and industry leaders in many sectors. The company is a pioneer of global distributed collaboration, with 1000\\+ colleagues in 70\\+ countries and very few office based roles. Teams meet two to four times yearly in person, in interesting locations around the world, to align on strategy and execution.
   

  

 The company is founder led, profitable and growing.
   

  

 We are hiring an
 **Embedded Linux Field Engineer for Devices/IoT** 
 to expand our reach in mission\\-critical industries such as Automotive, Medical Devices, Industrial Systems, Robotics, and Telco, as well as Consumer Electronics. We are looking for candidates who are accomplished Linux plumbers. If you are someone passionate about Linux, who knows the plumbing of the OS inside and out, who is proficient with distribution packaging, software design, system debugging, and bringing customer's ideas to life, then please keep on reading \\- this may be a uniquely exciting opportunity for you.
   

  

 The server edition of Ubuntu is already very widely used in connected devices and industrial PC's. Our edition of Ubuntu tailored for IoT, called Ubuntu Core, represents the state of the art in security and resilience for high end appliances and equipment. Our customers include global brands in consumer and industrial electronics as well as automotive and robotics. We continue to expand our range of offerings to bring our security, management and developer experience to the smallest Linux environments and devices. We recently added a real\\-time Linux capability and are working towards a range of certifications for these offerings. Together, this portfolio is Linux reinvented for optimal reliability, security, developer productivity and footprint.
   

  

 This career opportunity requires a unique blend of skills. Successful candidates will know Linux well and be proficient coders and scripters. They will have experience of low\\-level Linux boot, BIOS, EFI, Secure Boot, firmware, OS Distribution packaging and generally speaking embedded software development methodologies. They also enjoy the pace of change and diversity of client engagements with driven and ambitious technology entrepreneurs. Competitive, business\\-focused technologists at heart, they are also dedicated team players that take pride in team and company wins.
   

  

 We often say that our field engineers have 'the hardest job at Canonical' because customers can ask about any aspect of our solutions and products and expect a thoughtful, well\\-informed answer. We always want to do the best thing for our partners and customers, regardless of our company interests, and field engineers are the people we trust to ensure that is true.
   

  

 What your day will look like
   

  

* Engage customers during presales to gather requirements and explain our technology
* Elaborate solutions to be proposed to prospective clients
* Participate to the delivery of select projects related to Embedded Linux
* Convey market requirements to key stakeholders in our organization, and sometimes participate to the development or refining of generic solutions to unlock market potential
* Be both a customer advocate and a trusted advisor to Canonical


 What we are looking for in you
   

  

* Bachelors degree in Computer Science or related technical field
* Extensive Linux experience \\- Debian or Ubuntu preferred
* Distribution / Packaging experience (deb, rpm, ...)
* Solid embedded Linux experience (Yocto, Buildroot...)
* Fluency in at least one of Golang, Python, C, C\\+\\+, or Rust
* Professional written and spoken English in addition to the local language
* Excellent communication and presentation skills
* Result\\-oriented, ability to multi\\-task
* A personal drive to meet commitments
